what_it_does: Surveys external research patterns for compound AI systems and concurrent cognition, maps each pattern onto the project's swarm-based staged-stream design, notes empirical support and counter-evidence, and lists candidate refinements.
observations: Self-described as a 2026-06-07 outward research scout report with a staged-stream of parts produced by ~32 concurrent small-model role-runs.
Proposes a three-axis taxonomy: parallelism purpose (latency vs quality), number of models (same vs many), and interaction timing (aggregate-at-end vs interact-during-generation).

Names Compound AI Systems as the umbrella vocabulary, Mixture-of-Agents as the key empirical anchor, Self-MoA as counter-evidence that largely supports the one-model-many-draws choice, Blackboard architecture as a 40-year-old 1:1 ancestor, and Moshi as a rival voice shape.
Maps each external pattern to a project piece and also states a challenge or tradeoff for the project.
Includes a master table summarizing pattern, axes, core idea, project mapping, and tradeoff.
Explicitly marks reasoning beyond sources as [my inference].
Cites many source URLs, including some future-dated or not-yet-published arXiv identifiers.
Contains no code symbols or executable artifacts.
standouts: Self-MoA is framed as vindicating the choice of one resident model with many draws, while warning that low-quality role outputs should be gated rather than always injected.
The Blackboard architecture is presented as a near 1:1 match for the swarm address space and reactive scheduler.
Moshi full-duplex is highlighted as a rival shape that challenges the part-as-TTS cascade and suggests making the TTS/listening edge interruptible.
Mixture-of-Agents provides published evidence that composed smaller models can outperform a monolithic frontier model, but its aggregator was large, leaving the project's 4B aggregator unproven.
Skeleton-of-Thought's SoT-R router is used to argue that conditional staging via a mode-dial is mandatory.
conventions: Uses markdown headers and a structured survey format.
Cites sources inline and in a dedicated sources section.
Uses internal wiki-style links such as [[00-LANDSCAPE|concurrent-cognition]] and project-specific notation like swarm://<turn>/<role> and shape_for(mode).
Separates mapping sections from a final 'shapes that may be better' critique section.
concerns: Several cited sources are future-dated or from 2025/2026, so their real-world existence cannot be verified from the file alone.
The report mixes neutral survey language with advocacy for the project's design choices.
Many design implications are explicitly tagged as [my inference], indicating they are not directly sourced.
No methodology is given for how the ~32 concurrent role-runs were resolved or quality-controlled.
Some URLs may be inaccessible or speculative.
notes: A research scout report that grounds the project's concurrent-cognition swarm in named external patterns, empirical results, and honest risks, while proposing candidate refinements.
questions: Which of the future-dated or not-yet-published sources are real versus projected?
How exactly are the ~32 concurrent role-runs scheduled and their outputs resolved into the next part's context?
What is the specific resident 4B model and has its aggregator quality been measured?
How is the mode-dial implemented and which modes disable staging?
What does the referenced [[00-LANDSCAPE|concurrent-cognition]] file contain?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches stated purpose: it surveys the broader external design space, names patterns, places the project's design inside it, surfaces counter-evidence, and proposes candidate improvements.
apparent_themes: Concurrency for quality, not only latency
Streaming and real-time constraints shape architecture
Conditional staging controlled by a mode-dial
Tension between chunked voice cascades and full-duplex speech models
intention_signals: To justify the project's concurrent-cognition architecture by showing it has named precedents and empirical support in the literature, while openly flagging risks and possible improvements, and to carve out a claimed contribution around real-time streaming fusion of those precedents.
novelty: The explicit framing of a modern neural blackboard swarm feeding a part-by-part streaming reply on a single small resident model, with a mode-dial for conditional staging, as a fusion of older blackboard control, MoA/Self-MoA, and chunked-cascade voice latency hiding.
contribution: A structured literature map and vocabulary for the design space, plus a set of design implications and candidate refinements (selective role injection, interruptible TTS, conditional staging) that a fused system could keep as reference.
summary_for_embedding: A 2026-06-07 outward research scout report surveying external AI-system patterns relevant to a concurrent-cognition design, mapping Compound AI Systems, Mixture-of-Agents, Self-MoA, Skeleton-of-Thought, Blackboard architectures, and voice cascades/Moshi onto the project's swarm-based staged-stream architecture and identifying empirical supports, counter-evidence, and candidate refinements.
intention_for_embedding: To ground the project's concurrent-cognition design in named external precedents, empirical results, and honest risks while proposing refinements.
